mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate demand-pull inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or the signal is subsumed by broader GDP growth metrics.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 225, inflation expectations become unanchored and trigger aggressive monetary policy responses, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for nominal interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when tested against historical labor cycle data.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions signals peak economic activity and impending earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth signals fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the predictive relationship reverses when monetary policy remains accommodative.
